caveats: Blind snapshot labels reduce but do not eliminate selection bias.
No production ranking change is supported.

rows.reviewer_notes: Audio physical modeling with cross-domain mechanics flavor though bridge claim is moderate

rows.title: Modeling Time-Variant Responses of Optical Compressors With Selective State Space Models
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge between classic audio effects modeling and modern state-space ML methods

rows.title: Introducing the "Cockpit Party Problem": Blind Source Separation Enhances Aircraft Cockpit Speech Transcription

rows.reviewer_notes: Connects multimodal ML perception with music and audio interaction datasets

rows.title: Beyond a Western Center of Music Information Retrieval: A Bibliometric Analysis of the First 25 Years of ISMIR Authorship
rows.reviewer_notes: Bridges MIR community analysis with bibliometrics and diversity critique

rows.title: Optimising Computational Measures from Behavioural Data Predicts Perceived Consonance
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong link between behavioral perception experiments and computational consonance modeling

rows.title: Correction: Salsa, a Dataset for Beat Estimation in Salsa Music
rows.reviewer_notes: Correction notice is weak as a recommendation artifact despite relevant underlying dataset

rows.title: Cross‑Modal Approaches to Beat Tracking: A Case Study on Chopin Mazurkas
rows.reviewer_notes: Clear bridge across score-informed musicology and cross-modal beat tracking methods

rows.title: Predicting Eurovision Song Contest Results: A Hit Song Science Approach
rows.reviewer_notes: Connects music analytics with cultural event prediction but bridge strength is moderate

rows.title: Nuanced Music Emotion Recognition via a Semi-Supervised Multi-Relational Graph Neural Network
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge from affective neuroscience concepts to graph-based MIR modeling

rows.title: Estimation and Restoration of Unknown Nonlinear Distortion Using Diffusion
rows.reviewer_notes: Unexpected but meaningful bridge of diffusion restoration methods into audio distortion modeling

rows.title: A Similarity-Based Conditioning Method for Controllable Sound Effect Synthesis
rows.reviewer_notes: Bridges similarity retrieval ideas with controllable sound synthesis generation

rows.title: Neural Proxies for Sound Synthesizers: Learning Perceptually Informed Preset Representations
rows.reviewer_notes: Connects perceptual representation learning with practical synthesizer preset control

rows.title: Audio Signal Processing in the Artificial Intelligence Era: Challenges and Directions
rows.reviewer_notes: Broad 2025 JAES survey/position paper on AI for audio engineering; highly relevant to audio ML and useful as a bridge across audio DSP, ML systems, human-centered AI, real-time constraints, effects/synthesis/mixing/spatial/speech/music applications. Bridge-like, but partial because it is a broad field overview rather than a specific cross-cluster technical method.

rows.title: Ethical Dimensions of Music Information Retrieval Technology
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong MIR relevance; bridges MIR technology with ethics, socio-technical evaluation, and responsible system design.

rows.title: GiantMIDI-Piano: A Large-Scale MIDI Dataset for Classical Piano Music
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong dataset/resource paper; bridges MIR, symbolic music, piano transcription, and musicological analysis, but bridge aspect is dataset-driven rather than conceptual.

rows.title: The Sound Demixing Challenge 2023 - Music Demixing Track
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong audio-ML benchmark/challenge paper; connects source separation, standardized evaluation, datasets, and MIR practice, but mostly within one technical task area.

rows.title: On Evaluation of Inter- and Intra-Rater Agreement in Music Recommendation
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate: connects music recommendation, subjective similarity, user studies, and evaluation reliability.

rows.title: Diversity by Design in Music Recommender Systems
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate: connects music recommendation with diversity, value-sensitive design, fairness/user impact, and MIR methodology.

rows.title: Beyond a Western Center of Music Information Retrieval: A Bibliometric Analysis of the First 25 Years of ISMIR Authorship
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate; connects MIR authorship/history, geography, representation, and field-level bias rather than only technical MIR scoring.

rows.title: Perceptual and automated estimates of infringement in 40 music copyright cases
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate; connects MIR/audio similarity methods with legal copyright judgment and perceptual evaluation.

rows.title: Supervised Contrastive Models for Music Information Retrieval in Classical Persian Music
rows.reviewer_notes: Relevant MIR work applying supervised contrastive learning to Classical Persian music; bridges cultural repertoire and ML methods, but still mostly a focused retrieval/modeling task.

rows.title: Cross‑Modal Approaches to Beat Tracking: A Case Study on Chopin Mazurkas
rows.reviewer_notes: Relevant cross-modal beat-tracking case study connecting score/performance/musicological context with MIR, though narrower than the strongest bridge rows.

rows.reviewer_notes: Relevant sound-perception/audio-feature modeling work; bridges functional sound semantics and ML features, but may be slightly outside core MIR/music-paper focus.

rows.title: PiJAMA: Piano Jazz with Automatic MIDI Annotations
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong dataset/resource paper for jazz MIDI annotations; useful for MIR and symbolic music work, but bridge aspect is mostly dataset-driven.

rows.title: Intelligent User Interfaces for Music Discovery
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate; connects music discovery, recommender/interface design, user interaction, and MIR evaluation.

rows.title: Unveiling the Hierarchical Structure of Music by Multi-Resolution Community Detection
rows.reviewer_notes: Bridges network/community detection methods with music structure analysis; relevant and structurally bridge-like.

rows.title: Towards an 'Everything Corpus': A Framework and Guidelines for the Curation of More Comprehensive Multimodal Music Data
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate; connects multimodal music data, corpus design, curation methodology, and broader MIR infrastructure.

rows.title: ChoraleBricks: A Modular Multitrack Dataset for Wind Music Research
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong dataset/resource paper for wind music; useful for MIR and symbolic music work, but bridge aspect is mostly dataset-driven.

rows.title: Wagner Ring Dataset: A Complex Opera Scenario for Music Processing and Computational Musicology
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate; connects MIR, computational musicology, symbolic score representation, opera studies, and audio/recording analysis.

rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge candidate; connects ethnomusicology, cultural heritage, audio recordings, transcription/annotation, and computational analysis.

rows.title: JSD: A Dataset for Structure Analysis in Jazz Music
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong dataset/resource paper for jazz structure analysis; useful for MIR and annotation-driven tasks, but bridge aspect is mostly task-focused.

rows.title: Attend to Chords: Improving Harmonic Analysis of Symbolic Music Using Transformer-Based Models
rows.reviewer_notes: Relevant MIR method paper connecting harmonic analysis and transformer models, but primarily a focused task/method contribution.

rows.reviewer_notes: Bridges music reactive media with generative video and diffusion workflows though slightly less central to MIR or audio ranking

rows.reviewer_notes: Strong bridge across music mixing signal chain inference differentiable processors and ML audio production

rows.reviewer_notes: Useful audio engineering and perceptual evaluation paper but more within audio production than broadly cross-neighborhood

rows.reviewer_notes: Strong symbolic MIR dataset with broad downstream use for classical piano analysis and modeling; useful emerging/resource signal.

rows.title: Ethical Dimensions of Music Information Retrieval Technology
rows.reviewer_notes: Highly relevant MIR paper on ethical and socio-technical dimensions; useful discovery signal even though not new.

rows.title: The Sound Demixing Challenge 2023 - Music Demixing Track
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong benchmark/challenge paper for music source separation and robust demixing; useful for tracking current audio-ML evaluation movement.

rows.reviewer_notes: Strong recommendation-evaluation paper; human judgment reliability is directly useful for assessing music recommendation quality.

rows.title: Diversity by Design in Music Recommender Systems
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong music recommender systems paper connecting diversity and value-sensitive design; unusually useful beyond standard accuracy-focused recommendation work.

rows.title: Perceptual and automated estimates of infringement in 40 music copyright cases

rows.reviewer_notes: Strong MIR overview on music discovery interfaces

rows.title: DeepHits: A Multimodal CNN Approach to Hit Song Prediction

rows.abstract_preview: Hit Song Science aims to forecast a song's success before release and benefits from integrating signals beyond audio content alone. rows.title: Multitrack Music Transcription Based on Joint Learning of Onset and Frame Streams

rows.abstract_preview: Multitrack music transcription is the task of converting music recordings into symbolic music representations that are assigned to individual instruments. This task requires simultaneous transcription of note onset and offset events for individual instruments. In addition, the limited resources of many transcription datasets make multitrack music transcri...

rows.title: Diffusion-Based Denoising of Historical Recordings
rows.reviewer_notes: Diffusion restoration for historical music recordings is discovery friendly

In this work, a generative approach that adapts conditional diffusion sampling for removing perceptually distributed noi...

rows.title: Audio-visual archiving project in Dagbon of Northern Ghana during the period 1999-2010

rows.abstract_preview: This paper is a full chapter of my dissertation and elaborates on the constructivistic grounded theory as a field methodology during an audio-visual field research linked to an ethnographic study in Northern Ghana and in particular Dagbon society, seen from the angle of embodied music interaction and expressive timing, during the period 1999-2010. rows.reviewer_notes: OpenScore data infrastructure links scores crowdsourcing and Wikidata

We focus this discussion on the complex case of the 'OpenScore' initiative which creates digital encodings of musical scores under the CC0 licence for all use cases (musical, academic, and more...

rows.title: Evaluating Localization Accuracy of Dynamic Binaural Synthesis: Differences Between Individual, Estimated, and Generic HRTFs

rows.topics: Hearing Loss and Rehabilitation;Speech and Audio Processing;Noise Effects and Management

The experiment featured five different HRTF estimation methods, high-frequency...

rows.title: The Influence of Visual Cues on the Sound Externalization of Virtual Loudspeaker Setups
rows.reviewer_notes: AR visual cues and binaural reproduction bridge spatial audio modalities
rows.topics: Hearing Loss and Rehabilitation;Music Therapy and Health;Noise Effects and Management
rows.abstract_preview: Research has shown that visual cues influence the perceived sound externalization of binaural reproduction. However, it remains unclear whether providing a virtual visual representation of loudspeakers in addition to their binaural rendering influences their perceived externalization. Using augmented reality (AR), loudspeakers can be visualized within the...

rows.title: A Pilot Study of an Acoustic Calibration and Psychoacoustic Validation of a Binaural-Rendered Neuroengineering Laboratory
rows.reviewer_notes: Binaural lab calibration is relevant but peripheral neuroengineering audio
rows.topics: Vehicle Noise and Vibration Control;Noise Effects and Management;Hearing Loss and Rehabilitation
rows.abstract_preview: The increasing research in extended reality technologies has driven significant progress in developing virtual auditory experiments. These efforts aim to create immersive virtual environments, yet challenges such as room divergence-mismatches between virtual and physical auditory spaces-remain critical. This study focused on calibrating a binaural-rendere...

rows.title: Label-Efficient Social Noise Classification in Exceedance-Triggered Audio for Cost-Effective Source Tracing
rows.reviewer_notes: Urban social noise classification is audio adjacent but off product target
rows.abstract_preview: Identifying noise sources in exceedance-triggered audio is essential for targeted source tracing and sustainable urban social noise governance. While accurate models require massive labeled data, the acoustic complexity, high redundancy, and imbalanced class distributions of real-world recordings incur prohibitive manual annotation costs, hindering their...

rows.title: Wrap your head around it: algorithmic self-making and performances of taste on Spotify Wrapped
rows.reviewer_notes: Spotify Wrapped study informs platform taste and recommendation culture
rows.abstract_preview: This paper examines how Spotify Wrapped is perceived and understood by Brazilian users, exploring the algorithmic imaginaries (Bucher, 2017). that surround its appropriation in everyday life and how the datafication and the dashboarding of music consumption are incorporated into performances of taste (Airoldi, 2021; Prey, 2018). Focusing on Wrapped as an...

rows.title: New currents, old boundaries: exploring the relationship between streaming platforms and Afrikaans music
rows.reviewer_notes: Afrikaans streaming analysis is useful platform context but nontechnical
rows.abstract_preview: Abstract This article explores the impact of music streaming platforms on popular Afrikaans music. It sets recent technological developments in digital music distribution against the complex historical backdrop of the Afrikaans language and the histories of social marginalization affecting large parts of its linguistic communities. These patterns have sha...

rows.title: Analysis of Human Participants in the Journal of the Audio Engineering Society
rows.reviewer_notes: JAES human participant audit is relevant audio research meta context
rows.abstract_preview: This paper explores issues of equity, diversity, and inclusion in the Audio Engineering Society. It provides an overview of recent initiatives and publications on the participation of various groups in the audio industry and the Audio Engineering Society community. It also discusses the concept of justice in human research and its relevance to the recruit...

rows.title: A Multidimensional MIR Analysis of Acoustic, Linguistic and Cultural Gaps Between Maskandi and Western Music Genres
rows.reviewer_notes: Cross-cultural MIR and NLP gaps are highly relevant and discovery friendly
rows.abstract_preview: Contemporary Music Information Retrieval (MIR) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems are increasingly applied to diverse musical traditions, yet they are largely grounded in Western musical and linguistic assumptions. This study examines whether commonly used MIR features and multilingual NLP models adequately represent the acoustic, linguistic, a...

rows.title: Analysis of an intelligent piano music transcription model by deep reinforcement learning

rows.abstract_preview: To improve the accuracy of automatic piano music transcription in complex environments, a recognition system applicable to practical scenarios such as music education assistance and intelligent performance analysis was developed.First, audio features were extracted using Log-Mel spectrograms, combined with data augmentation and adaptive pitch normalisatio...

rows.title: [Paper] TPSMG: Text-Controllable Polyphonic Symbolic Music Generation
rows.reviewer_notes: Text controllable symbolic generation links language diffusion and MIDI
rows.abstract_preview: We propose a novel text-controllable polyphonic symbolic music generation method based on diffusion models. rows.title: Uncovering Hidden Themes in Indie Music: Crisp-Dm Guided LDA Topic Modeling on a Kaggle-Based Lyric Generation Dataset
rows.reviewer_notes: Lyric topic modeling is relevant but weak and predictable
rows.abstract_preview: The development of music has produced many works in the form of data, especially lyrical data, which provide insight into the semantic structure of music. This study explores latent thematic patterns in the indie lyric dataset from Kaggle by applying Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA), which is the first LDA study of indie music lyrics in the Indonesian co...

rows.title: Polyphonic music melody generation model based on inverse reinforcement learning algorithm
rows.reviewer_notes: Inverse reinforcement learning for symbolic generation is relevant
rows.abstract_preview: Automated polyphonic music generation remains a challenging task due to the difficulty in designing reward functions and modelling long-range dependencies.To address this, this paper proposes a polyphonic music generation model based on generative adversarial imitation learning.Our model employs a Gated Transformer-XL as its core to effectively capture in...

rows.title: Data-Driven Uncertainty Modeling for Robust Feedback Active Noise Control in Headphones
rows.reviewer_notes: Headphone ANC uncertainty modeling is useful but narrow audio engineering
rows.topics: Advanced Adaptive Filtering Techniques;Control Systems and Identification;Vehicle Noise and Vibration Control

Although feedback control offers an effective way to implement ANC, it is restricted by uncertainty of the controlled system (e.g., that arises from differing wearing situations). Widely used unstructured models that capture these variations ten...

rows.title: Disc and Ring Exciter Shape Effects on Flat-Panel Loudspeakers
rows.reviewer_notes: Flat panel loudspeaker exciter modeling is relevant but very hardware narrow
rows.topics: Acoustic Wave Phenomena Research;Speech and Audio Processing;Acoustic Wave Resonator Technologies

Simulations normally treat the exciter as acting like a point force, but in reality, the ring or disc shape can have a significant influence on the frequency response of the plate at middle and high fre...

rows.title: Bayesian-Optimized Ensemble Learning for Music Popularity Prediction with Shapley-Based Interpretability

In this study, music popularity prediction is formulated as a supervised regression problem, and six widely-used tree ensemble models (Random Forest, XGBoost...

rows.title: Antiderivative Antialiasing for Chebyshev Based Generalized Hammerstein Models
rows.reviewer_notes: Digital audio effects antialiasing is relevant DSP for music technology
rows.abstract_preview: Antiderivative antialiasing (ADAA) has proven to be an effective approach for reducing aliasing in mathematically defined nonlinear functions. This paper explores the application of ADAA to Chebyshev-based generalized Hammerstein models, which are utilized for blackbox modeling of nonlinearities in digital audio effects. The Chebyshev-based model eliminat...

rows.title: Integrated virtual reality and musical biofeedback for intensity-guided training on stationary cycling: a comparative feasibility study
rows.reviewer_notes: Musical biofeedback in VR rehab is peripheral but strong interdisciplinary bridge
rows.abstract_preview: Abstract Motor rehabilitation requiring sustained physical exercise faces poor adherence in neurological populations due to insufficient supervision and monotony. While virtual reality and musical biofeedback independently improve engagement and motivation, their comparative and combined impact on intensity control strategies during high-intensity interva...

Beyond the absorption and internalisation of the score (note-learning, memorisation, addressing technical issues), a...

rows.title: Experimental investigation of lip-specific mouthpiece forces and pressure parameters in trombone performance under realistic playing conditions

rows.abstract_preview: Mastering brass playing depends on the fine control of the embouchure-the coordinated interaction between lips, mouthpiece, and airflow. While teachers and performers have long emphasised this balance, objective data on how lip forces and pressures interact during real performance are scarce. rows.title: BEATSCORE: Beat-Synchronous Contrastive Alignment and Event-Centric Grading for Long-Term Sports Assessment

rows.abstract_preview: Long-term sports assessment is a challenging task in video understanding, since it requires judging subtle movement variations over minutes and evaluating action-music coordination. rows.title: A music source separation method integrating time-frequency decoupling and mamba-based state space modeling

However, existing separation models often suffer from structural limitations such as reliance on a single modeling path, entangled time-frequency representations, and di...

rows.title: Multimodal segmentation and labeling of lecture recordings for educational content retrieval

Lecture recordings, in particular, have become a widely used resource, offering students the ability to revisit class content at their own pace. However, the sheer volume and leng...

rows.title: How Far Can a U-Net Go? An Empirical Analysis of Music Source Separation Performance

rows.title: MMAudioSep: Taming Video-to-Audio Generative Model Towards Video/Text-Queried Sound Separation
rows.reviewer_notes: Video and text queried sound separation is a strong multimodal audio bridge
rows.abstract_preview: We introduce MMAudioSep, a generative model for video/text-queried sound separation that is founded on a pretrained video-to-audio model. By leveraging knowledge about the relationship between video/text and audio learned through a pretrained audio generative model, we can train the model more efficiently, i.e., the model does not need to be trained from...

rows.title: The Sahelian Factor as a Structural Key Component in the Contemporary Idioms of Music-Making in Dagbon
rows.reviewer_notes: Dagbon musicology is culturally rich but lacks product relevant tech angle
rows.abstract_preview: In this paper, we present the theoretical framework of the Sahelian factor as a structural research model for analysing the music and dance of Northern Ghana. Drawing on longitudinal audiovisual fieldwork in Dagbon and a dialogue with local musicians, stakeholders, music journalists, radio presenters, and the Dagbon community in Tamale, this model offers...

rows.title: A scoping review of music-based digital therapeutics for stress, anxiety, and depression
rows.reviewer_notes: Music digital therapeutics review bridges music health and apps
rows.abstract_preview: Rising rates of stress, anxiety, and depression-fueled by rapid sociocultural and economic shifts, digital overexposure, and the lasting impact of COVID-19-are accelerating investment in scalable tools aimed at enhancing resilience and wellbeing. Music-based digital therapeutics (MDTs) hold promise given music's unique ability to modulate core dimensions...

rows.title: The use of background music in educational settings
rows.reviewer_notes: Background music in classrooms is music adjacent education research
rows.abstract_preview: This study is a theoretical review that examines, from a holistic perspective, the effects of background music used in classroom settings on students' cognitive, affective, and social learning processes. Drawing on contemporary research in educational psychology, environmental structuring, learning theories, and special education, the contributions of bac...

rows.title: Practice makes perfect sense to me: a retrospective analysis of recommending independent skills practice in music therapy for military-connected individuals
rows.reviewer_notes: Music therapy practice study is clinical and not useful for discovery
rows.abstract_preview: Introduction Therapeutic disciplines have researched the use of independent skills practice (i.e., homework) and its impact on patient outcomes, but research on this topic in music therapy has been limited and contradictory. Methods This retrospective, exploratory study used a correlational approach to explore the use of recommending independent skills pr...

rows.title: Praat Audiotools: An offline analysis-resynthesis toolkit for experimental composition
rows.reviewer_notes: Praat repurposed for electroacoustic composition is useful and unexpected
rows.abstract_preview: Abstract Praat AudioTools is an open-source library of over 300 scripts that transforms the phonetic analysis software Praat (Boersma and Weenink) into an offline, object-centric laboratory for electroacoustic composition. While Praat is the standard tool for linguistic analysis, its potential for musical creation has remained largely untapped due to its...

rows.title: Artificial Intelligence in Music: A Bibliometric and Systematic Review of Creation, Performance, and Education
rows.reviewer_notes: AI music review is relevant overview but broad and predictable
rows.abstract_preview: Abstract The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into the music domain has catalyzed a transformative shift in how music is composed, performed, and taught. This paper introduces and frames the concept of music intelligence and employs bibliometric and systematic review methodologies to comprehensively analyze music intelligence. Music intelligenc...

rows.title: Music emotion classification based on random swap algorithm
rows.reviewer_notes: Music emotion clustering is relevant but narrow and modest
rows.abstract_preview: We performed an unsupervised cluster analysis of emotions in music. We considered ten distinct perceived emotions (happy, sad, amusing, annoying, anxious, relaxing, dreamy, energizing, joyful, and neutral) from four genres (rock, pop, classical, and electronic). We first clustered emotions based on their co-occurrence across songs, revealing that the emot...

rows.title: Emo-Tune: Harnessing Emotion-based Music for Patient Wellness
rows.reviewer_notes: Emotion based recommendation for wellness is relevant but predictable
rows.abstract_preview: Music has consistently been a favored means of conveying and comprehending human emotions, serving as a powerful medium of expression. It has profound impact on human emotions, and our work aims to leverage this connection to enhance the human experience. This paper aims to enhance emotional well­being by identifying an individual's emotional state and pl...

rows.title: Enhancing long-term structure in symbolic music generation via a cascaded Skeleton-to-texture framework
rows.reviewer_notes: Structure aware symbolic generation addresses a meaningful music AI problem
rows.abstract_preview: In the field of symbolic music generation, maintaining macro-structural coherence and preventing logical drift within long sequences remains a critical challenge. Traditional autoregressive models primarily rely on implicit probabilistic statistics to capture contextual dependencies. Consequently, they often struggle to retain memory of initial musical mo...

rows.title: Constructing Composite Features for Interpretable Music-Tagging
rows.reviewer_notes: Interpretable music tagging features are directly useful for MIR
rows.abstract_preview: Combining multiple audio features can improve the performance of music tagging, but common deep learning-based feature fusion methods often lack interpretability. To address this problem, we propose a Genetic Programming (GP) pipeline that automatically evolves composite features by mathematically combining base music features, thereby capturing synergist...

rows.title: GuitarFlow: Realistic Electric Guitar Synthesis From Tablatures via Flow Matching and Style Transfer
rows.reviewer_notes: Controllable guitar synthesis from tablature is highly product relevant
rows.abstract_preview: Music generation in the audio domain using artificial intelligence (AI) has witnessed steady progress in recent years. However for some instruments, particularly the guitar, controllable instrument synthesis remains limited in expressivity. We introduce GuitarFlow, a model with a high degree of controllability designed specifically for electric guitar syn...

rows.title: Text2midi-InferAlign: Improving Symbolic Music Generation with Inference-Time Alignment
rows.reviewer_notes: Inference time alignment for text to MIDI links audio rewards and symbolic music
rows.abstract_preview: We present Text2midi-InferAlign, a novel technique for improving symbolic music generation at inference time. Our method leverages text-to-audio and music-structural alignment rewards to guide the generation process, encouraging consistency with the input caption. Specifically, we introduce two objective scores: a text-audio consistency score measuring rh...

rows.title: Driven by engagement and social identity: refining the SOR framework to explore digital music consumption behaviors among Chinese undergraduate students on short video platforms
rows.reviewer_notes: Short video music consumption informs recommendation behavior but is sociological
rows.abstract_preview: Grounded in the Stimulus-Organism-Response (SOR) framework, this study refines the model within the context of short video platforms (SVPs) and digital music consumption. By incorporating social identity as a context-specific "Organism" mediator alongside music recommendation satisfaction, we explore the dual psychological pathways-individual and social-t...

rows.title: Sentiment-Based Music Recommendation System using Natural Language Processing for Emotion-aware Song Suggestions
rows.reviewer_notes: Sentiment based music recommendation is relevant but conventional
rows.abstract_preview: Music plays a vital role in influencing emotions, mood, and mental health. However, classical music recommendation systems mostly rely on listening to history, genre preference, or popularity, ignoring the listener's mood. Motivation to go further in this area gave birth to this study, where a new sentiment-based music recommendation system is being desig...

rows.title: Characteristics of Personal Sound and Noise Exposure at Large-Scale Music Events
rows.reviewer_notes: Personal exposure at music events is useful but hearing safety focused
rows.abstract_preview: Regulations governing sound exposure at amplified-music events aim to safeguard audience's hearing. However, current sound monitoring practices often rely on a single measurement position that is considered representative of individual exposure. Because this standard practice overlooks crucial inter-subject variation in sound exposure, this study analyzes...

rows.title: Deepaq: A Perceptual Audio Quality Metric Based on Foundational Models and Weakly Supervised Learning
rows.reviewer_notes: Foundation model audio quality metric is highly relevant for evaluation
rows.abstract_preview: This paper presents the Deep learning-based Perceptual Audio Quality metric (DeePAQ) for evaluating general audio quality. Our approach leverages metric learning together with the music foundation model MERT, guided by surrogate labels, to construct an embedding space that captures distortion intensity in general audio. To the best of our knowledge, DeePA...

rows.title: SingMOS-Pro: An Comprehensive Benchmark For Singing Quality Assessment
rows.reviewer_notes: Singing quality benchmark supports music generation evaluation
rows.abstract_preview: Singing voice generation progresses rapidly, yet evaluating singing quality remains a critical challenge. Human subjective assessment, typically in the form of listening tests, is costly and time consuming, while existing objective metrics capture only limited perceptual aspects. In this work, we introduce SingMOS-Pro, a dataset for automatic singing qual...

rows.title: TuFoC: regional classification of Turkish folk music recordings using deep learning on Mel spectrograms
rows.reviewer_notes: Regional Turkish folk audio classification is useful underrepresented MIR
rows.abstract_preview: The regional classification of Turkish folk music remains a relatively unexplored domain in music information retrieval, particularly when leveraging raw audio signals for deep learning. This study addresses this gap by investigating how modern deep learning architectures can effectively classify regional folk recordings from limited original data using c...

rows.title: Modeling the Effect of Eddy Currents on the Inductance of Loudspeaker Motors Using the Method of Lines
rows.reviewer_notes: Loudspeaker motor physics is relevant audio hardware but very narrow
rows.topics: Acoustic Wave Phenomena Research;Advanced Adaptive Filtering Techniques;Electromagnetic Compatibility and Noise Suppression
rows.abstract_preview: This paper presents a way of modeling the effect of eddy currents on the inductance of a loudspeaker coil over a wide frequency bandwidth without the use of fractional derivatives or Foster/Cauer networks. The model presented is a physics-based model, which is derived from Maxwell's equations and discretized in space. The model is compared with finite ele...

rows.title: ECHO: Frequency-Aware Hierarchical Encoding for Variable-Length Signals
rows.reviewer_notes: General signal foundation model may transfer to audio but is not music specific
rows.abstract_preview: Pre-trained foundation models have demonstrated remarkable success in audio, vision and language, yet their potential for general machine signal modeling with arbitrary sampling rates-covering acoustic, vibration, and other industrial sensor data-remains under-explored. In this work, we propose a novel foundation model ECHO that integrates an advanced ban...

rows.title: HashWave: blockchain-powered perceptual hashing for resilient audio piracy detection against signal-processing attacks in decentralized networks
rows.reviewer_notes: Audio piracy hashing with blockchain is niche but cross domain music tech
rows.abstract_preview: Audio piracy detection is increasingly complex in decentralised distribution settings, where mainstream approaches fail to ensure robustness, verifiability, or computational efficiency. Conventional Digital Rights Management (DRM) systems mainly enforce licensed access, but once content is copied or redistributed outside their control they offer little pr...

rows.title: Tonal Mode Detection Based on the Triple Composite Signature of Fifths
rows.reviewer_notes: Tonal mode detection is useful symbolic MIR and music theory algorithm work
rows.abstract_preview: In this article, a novel algorithm for tonal mode classification utilizing the signature of fifths is presented. The proposed method is an extension of previously developed algorithms used for determining the key and key signature within the Western tonal system. The core idea of the algorithm is to analyze the entire piece as well as its most important e...

rows.title: CollabScore, a collaborative system for OMR validation and correction
rows.reviewer_notes: Collaborative OMR correction is useful music data infrastructure
rows.abstract_preview: We describe a collaborative system, CollabScore, aiming at validating and correcting music scores produced by an optical recognition process. Such output are notably faulty and require a correction step. While a general-purpose engraver could used for that purpose, this solution lacks scalability and does not directly addresses the symbol identification w...

rows.title: Transkripcija kitarskih tablatur z globokimi transformerskimi modeli
rows.reviewer_notes: Transformer guitar tablature transcription is a relevant MIR niche
rows.abstract_preview: Automatic guitar tablature transcription remains an under-explored problem in automatic music transcription due to limited real-world annotated data with fretboard annotations and the instrument-specific complexity of assigning tones to a specific fretting position. In this thesis, a transformerbased sequence-to-sequence model for direct audio-to-tablatur...

rows.title: CABIF-Net: Robust Confidence-Based Audio-Visual Fusion for Fine-Grained Bird Recognition
rows.reviewer_notes: Bird recognition is off slice despite audio visual methods
rows.abstract_preview: Fine-grained bird identification is crucial for ecosystem monitoring, species conservation, and habitat assessment. However, in real-world environments, there are challenges such as imbalances in modality quality and interference from background noise. To improve fine-grained audio-visual bird classification under heterogeneous modality conditions, we pro...

rows.title: Signal Dependencies of the Immersion Sweet Area in Multichannel Loudspeaker Reproduction
rows.reviewer_notes: Multichannel sweet area study is relevant spatial audio but narrow
rows.topics: Speech and Audio Processing;Acoustic Wave Phenomena Research
rows.abstract_preview: Multichannel loudspeaker systems are generally optimized for a particular listening position-the so-called sweet spot. A sweet area around this position may be defined within which the reproduced sound field is judged to be similar-physically or perceptually-to what is observed in the sweet spot itself. Here, the sweet area is defined in terms of physical...

rows.title: Numerical Noise in Fixed-Pole Parallel and Kautz Filters
rows.reviewer_notes: Audio filter numerical noise is relevant DSP but not discovery friendly
rows.abstract_preview: In audio filtering, Kautz and fixed-pole parallel filters are commonly used, as they have the ability to approximate the frequency resolution of hearing. However, infinite impulse response filters are susceptible to numerical noise, as quantization happens in their feedback loop. This is especially severe in systems where only fixed-point arithmetic is av...

rows.title: Auditory Analysis of Tabla solo performances A study of Gharana specific characteristics
rows.reviewer_notes: Tabla gharana analysis is culturally useful but limited technical framing
rows.abstract_preview: Tabla is a pounding instrument in Hindustani classical music tradition. Tabla learning and presentation in the Indian landmass is based on stylistic schools called Gharana. Each Gharana is attributed by its main style of playing skills, act of Tabla beats, repertoire, compositions and improvisations. Recognizing the Gharana from a Tabla presentation is ma...

rows.title: Automated detection of stereotyped animal sounds using data augmentation and transfer learning
rows.reviewer_notes: Animal sound detection is audio ML but not music technology discovery
rows.abstract_preview: Deep learning-based automated call detectors offer a solution to the labour- and time-intensive problem of isolating animal sounds within the enormous datasets generated by passive acoustic monitoring (PAM). Broad adoption of deep learning systems in PAM has been hampered by the need for large, labelled training datasets, which do not exist for species wh...

rows.title: Multi-track symbolic music generation algorithm based on diffusion model and music structure constraints
rows.reviewer_notes: Diffusion symbolic multitrack generation is directly relevant music AI
rows.abstract_preview: When generating symbolic music, people sometimes encounter problems such as unclear structure or mismatch between tracks.Accordingly, this study proposes a multi-track symbolic music generation method to solve the problems of melodic fragmentation, uneven rhythm, and multi-track conflicts in traditional music generation.Experiments on several music datase...

rows.reviewer_notes: Speech/acoustics measurement paper; weak fit for bridge recommendation in MIR/audio-ML context.

rows.reviewer_notes: Biomedical nanocarrier review is off-slice for emerging MIR/audio-ML.

rows.reviewer_notes: Oncology music-intervention guideline is off-slice for emerging audio-ML/MIR.

rows.reviewer_notes: Strong undercited symbolic-music resource; very large MIDI dataset with expressive-performance features is likely useful for MIR

rows.title: CCMusic: An Open and Diverse Database for Chinese Music Information Retrieval Research
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong low-cite discovery; open Chinese music MIR database broadens cultural coverage and includes unified datasets/evaluation for reproducible Chinese music MIR.

rows.title: Introducing the "Cockpit Party Problem": Blind Source Separation Enhances Aircraft Cockpit Speech Transcription
rows.reviewer_notes: Interesting low-cite audio source-separation application to cockpit voice recorder transcription

rows.title: Barwise Music Structure Analysis with the Correlation Block-Matching Segmentation Algorithm
rows.reviewer_notes: Relevant low-cite MIR structure-analysis paper; useful algorithmic contribution for music segmentation

rows.title: The Sound Demixing Challenge 2023 - Music Demixing Track
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong audio-ML benchmark/challenge paper; useful for tracking source-separation evaluation and robust music demixing systems.

rows.title: Perceptual and automated estimates of infringement in 40 music copyright cases
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong undercited discovery; connects MIR similarity methods

rows.title: PiJAMA: Piano Jazz with Automatic MIDI Annotations
rows.reviewer_notes: Useful low-cite dataset/resource paper for jazz piano MIDI annotations and symbolic MIR; valuable

rows.title: Issues and Challenges of Audio Technologies for the Musical Metaverse
rows.reviewer_notes: Clearly relevant and undercited relative to topical value

rows.title: Supervised Contrastive Models for Music Information Retrieval in Classical Persian Music
rows.reviewer_notes: Valuable and less-common regional repertoire focus for MIR

rows.title: RWC Revisited: Towards a Community-Driven MIR Corpus
rows.reviewer_notes: Important corpus stewardship topic with high utility

rows.title: Inferring Communities of Medieval Music Manuscripts Using Stochastic Block Models
rows.reviewer_notes: Interesting but somewhat specialized for core product discovery use

rows.title: Investigating Auditory-Visual Perception Using Multi-Modal Neural Networks with the SoundActions Dataset
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong multimodal direction and currently low-cite

rows.title: Beyond a Western Center of Music Information Retrieval: A Bibliometric Analysis of the First 25 Years of ISMIR Authorship
rows.reviewer_notes: Helpful community-level evidence for inclusive corpus strategy

rows.title: Improving Motif Discovery of Symbolic Polyphonic Music with Motif Note Identification
rows.reviewer_notes: Solid symbolic-MIR method contribution deserving visibility

rows.title: Optimising Computational Measures from Behavioural Data Predicts Perceived Consonance
rows.reviewer_notes: Good psychoacoustic-computational link that is currently undercited

rows.title: Smartwatch-Based Audio-Gestural Insights in Violin Bow Stroke Analyses
rows.reviewer_notes: Interesting modality but narrower impact for broad discovery

rows.title: MGPHot: A Dataset of Musicological Annotations for Popular Music (1958-2022)
rows.reviewer_notes: High-value annotations dataset likely under-recognized currently

rows.title: Correction: Salsa, a Dataset for Beat Estimation in Salsa Music
rows.reviewer_notes: Correction notice is weak as recommendation despite relevant base topic

rows.title: Cross‑Modal Approaches to Beat Tracking: A Case Study on Chopin Mazurkas
rows.reviewer_notes: Methodologically strong and undercited in cross-modal beat tracking

rows.title: Predicting Eurovision Song Contest Results: A Hit Song Science Approach
rows.reviewer_notes: Interesting but somewhat peripheral to core MIR operations

rows.title: Nuanced Music Emotion Recognition via a Semi-Supervised Multi-Relational Graph Neural Network
rows.reviewer_notes: Strong and relevant MER method that is low-citation so far

rows.title: The Story Behind the Real World Computing Music Database: An Interview with Masataka Goto
rows.reviewer_notes: Contextual interview useful historically but lower direct recommendation value

rows.title: Multimodal Raga Classification from Vocal Performances with Disentanglement and Contrastive Loss
rows.reviewer_notes: Culturally broader MIR application and method is especially discovery-worthy

rows.title: ImproVision Equilibrium: Toward Multimodal Musical Human-Machine Interaction
rows.reviewer_notes: Novel multimodal HMI framing with potential high upside for discovery

rows.title: 3D Microphone Array Comparison Part 2: Elicitation of Salient Perceptual Attributes
rows.reviewer_notes: Technically relevant though narrower than core MIR retrieval interests

rows.title: High-Resolution Directivity Measurements of an Artificial Head and Mouth Shaped to Three Vowels
rows.reviewer_notes: Acoustics relevance is real but less central to MIR discovery goals

rows.title: Improved Real-Time Six-Degrees-of-Freedom Dynamic Auralization Through Nonuniformly Partitioned Convolution
rows.reviewer_notes: Good practical spatial-audio rendering work with low current citations
